page: Vultr vcg-a16-3c-32g-8vram is a Cloud GPU server configured with an x86_64 Intel Broadwell architecture. It features 3 shared physical cores operating at 2.0 GHz, paired with 32.0 GB of system memory, which equates to 10.67 GB of RAM per core. The hardware profile includes a fractional NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPU allocation providing 8 GB of dedicated VRAM. Local storage is supported by a 170 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks reveal a clear contrast between CPU-bound tasks and GPU-accelerated workloads. The shared Broadwell CPU shows weak multi-core performance in stress-ng and PassMark tests, alongside weak memory bandwidth for small cached blocks. However, the integrated NVIDIA A16 GPU delivers top-tier performance for LLM inference prompt processing, reaching 8612.59 tokens per second on a small 135M model and 600.63 tokens per second on a medium 7B model. Text generation speeds are also strong, achieving 27.65 tokens per second on the 7B model.

Given the combination of a shared, older CPU architecture and a dedicated fractional GPU, this instance offers a cost-effective entry point for GPU-accelerated workloads without the expense of a full dedicated GPU. It is poorly suited for CPU-heavy multi-threaded computing, database operations, or high-throughput web serving. Instead, the server is optimized for lightweight LLM inference, model prototyping, and GPU-assisted development tasks that benefit from the 8 GB VRAM and NVMe storage.

The server is equipped with a fractional NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPU providing 8 GB of VRAM. Benchmark data indicates weak multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance, but top-tier results in LLM inference prompt processing, reaching 600.63 tokens per second on a medium 7B model. This hardware profile offers a cost-efficient entry point for GPU-accelerated tasks, avoiding the cost of a full GPU. It is best suited for lightweight LLM inference, model prototyping, and development workloads rather than CPU-intensive applications.
og_description: Vultr vcg-a16-3c-32g-8vram is a Cloud GPU server featuring 3 shared Intel cores, 32 GB RAM, a 170 GB NVMe SSD, and a fractional NVIDIA A16 GPU with 8 GB VRAM, optimized for lightweight LLM inference.

tagline: A budget-friendly fractional GPU instance featuring an NVIDIA Ampere A16 and NVMe storage for lightweight machine learning inference and prototyping.
bullet_points: Fractional NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPU with 8 GB of dedicated VRAM
3 shared Intel Broadwell vCPUs and 32.0 GB of system memory
170 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier performance for LLM inference prompt processing
Cost-efficient entry point for GPU-accelerated development and prototyping
